1.9: I am alive ; Oath formula. See Isaiah ,
1.5: ) this would be an allusion to the platform where the god Dagon sat enthroned.
1.11: On my holy mountain ; That is to say, in my temple. The Jews took excessive pride in the beauty of their temple; Jeremiah (see Jeremiah ,
1.15: will whistle ...as a sign of contempt.
1.16: High niches ; That is to say, the towers that were erected at the corners of the walls.
1.18: . — The prophecy contained in this verse and the following ones had a beginning of fulfillment at the return from the Babylonian captivity; but there are features which apply some to the first, others to the last coming of Jesus Christ.
1.19: the one who limps, the house of Judah, which sought to combine the worship of the Lord with that of idols. the rejected one, perhaps the house of Israel, which had been repudiated because of its infidelities. Compare to Micah
1.10: These rivers from Ethiopia not are other than the Nile divided into its seven branches at its mouth in the Mediterranean.

2.1: Gather yourselves, turn inward, shameless race,
2.2: before the decree has given birth, before the day has passed like chaff; before the fierce anger of the Lord comes upon you, before the day of the Lord's anger comes upon you.
2.3: Seek the Lord, all you humble of the earth, who have obeyed his law; seek righteousness, seek humility. Perhaps you will be safe on the day of the Lord’s anger.,
2.4: for Gaza will be abandoned and Ashkelon reduced to a desert, Azotus will be driven out at midday and Hararon will be uprooted.
2.5: Woe to the inhabitants of the sea region, to the nation of Crete! The word of the Lord is spoken against you, Canaan, land of the Philistines: «And I will destroy you so that no inhabitants remain.»
2.6: The sea region will become pastures, shepherds' caves and sheep parks.
2.7: This will be a territory for the remnant of the house of Judah; they will graze their flocks there; in the evening they will take up residence in the houses of Ashkelon, for the Lord their God will visit them and restore them.
2.8: I have heard the insults of Moab and the outrages of the sons of Ammon, who insulted my people and expanded themselves at the expense of its borders.
2.9: Therefore, as surely as I live, declares the Lord Almighty, the God of Israel, Moab will become like Sodom and the Ammonites like Gomorrah: a place overgrown with nettles, a salt mine, a desolate wasteland forever. The remnant of my people will plunder it, and the survivors of my nation will inherit it.
2.10: This will happen to them because of their pride, because they have been contemptuous and insolent towards the people of the Lord of hosts.
2.11: The Lord will be an object of terror to them because he will destroy all the gods of the earth, and before him will bow down, each from his own place, the inhabitants of all the islands of the nations.
2.12: You too, Ethiopians, will be pierced by my sword.
2.13: He will also extend his hand against the north, he will destroy Assur and he will make Nineveh a desolate wasteland, as barren as the desert.
2.14: In its midst, flocks and animals of every kind will find shelter; even the pelican and the hedgehog will make their home in its ruins. Songs will be heard from the windows, desolation will be on the threshold, for the cedar woodwork has been stripped bare.
2.15: This is the joyous city, seated in assurance, which said in its heart: «I am, and there is no one besides me.» How has it become a desolate place, a dwelling for beasts? Whoever passes over it will hiss and wave their hand.

3.1: Woe to the rebellious and defiled one, to the city of oppression.
3.2: She listened to no voice, she did not accept any warning; she did not trust in the Lord, she did not draw near to her God.
3.3: Her princes in her midst are roaring lions, her judges are evening wolves, who leave nothing for the morning.
3.4: His prophets are boastful, treacherous men; his priests profane holy things, they violate the law.
3.5: The Lord is righteous in her midst; he does no wickedness; every morning he brings forth his judgment, to give light, never failing, but the wicked know no shame.
3.6: I have exterminated nations; their towers have been destroyed; I have devastated their streets, so that no one can pass through them anymore; their cities have been laid waste, so that no one remains, no inhabitant.
3.7: I said, "At least you will fear me, you will accept the warning, and his dwelling will not be destroyed according to all that I have decided concerning him." But they were only more eager to pervert all their actions.
3.8: Therefore, wait for me, declares the Lord, for the day when I rise up to seize the spoils, for my purpose is to gather the nations, to assemble the kingdoms, to pour out on them my fury, all the fierceness of my anger. For, in the fire of my jealousy, the whole earth shall be devoured.
3.9: For then I will give pure lips to the peoples, so that all of them will call on the name of the Lord and serve him with one accord.
3.10: From beyond the rivers of Ethiopia, my worshippers, my scattered people, will bring my offering.
3.11: On that day you will no longer be ashamed of all your deeds by which you have sinned against me, for then I will remove from among you those who rejoiced arrogantly, and you will no longer boast on my holy mountain.
3.12: I will leave in your midst a humble and lowly people, who will trust in the name of the Lord.
3.13: The remnant of Israel will not commit iniquity, they will not speak lies, and deceitful tongues will no longer be found in their mouths. For they will be able to graze and lie down, and no one will disturb them.
3.14: Shout for joy, daughter of Zion. Shout aloud, Israel. Rejoice and exult with all your heart, daughter of Jerusalem.
3.15: The Lord has withdrawn the judgments against you; he has turned away your enemy. The Lord, the King of Israel, is in your midst; you will see no more harm.
3.16: On that day it will be said to Jerusalem: Do not fear, Zion, lest your hands hang limp.
3.17: The Lord your God is in your midst, a mighty savior. He will rejoice greatly over you; he will be silent in his love; he will exult over you with shouts of joy.
3.18: I will gather those who are in sorrow, deprived of solemn feasts; for they were your people, reproach weighs upon them.
3.19: “At that time I will put an end to all your oppressors; I will save the lame and gather the rejected, and I will make them glorious and renowned in all the countries where they have been despised.”.
3.20: At that time I will bring you back; and at the time I gather you together, I will make you renowned and glorious among all the peoples of the earth, when I end your captivity before your eyes, says the Lord.
